name: Chocolate Hazelnut Surprise Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Spread hazelnuts on a baking pan and heat until skins are cracked and nuts are toasted.
3. Transfer the hazelnuts to a wire mesh strainer, protecting your hand with a potholder, and rub them briskly to remove skins and cool.
4. Set aside 12 whole hazelnuts; coarsely chop and reserve for garnish. Finely chop the remaining hazelnuts in a food processor with 1 tablespoon of sugar and set aside.
5. Lightly oil and flour a 10-inch springform pan. Shake out excess flour.
6. Beat the whole eggs and egg whites in a large mixing bowl until frothy.
7. Gradually beat in the remaining 3/4 cup sugar. Continue beating until the mixture is pale yellow in color.
8. At the lowest speed, beat in the oil in a slow, steady stream.
9. Add the coffee and vanilla. Stir flour, cocoa powder, and salt together in a large bowl.
10. Stir in the finely chopped hazelnut-sugar mixture.
11. Add the egg mixture and gently fold until thoroughly blended.
12. Pour into the prepared pan.
13. Bake for about 35 minutes until the edges pull away from the sides of the pan and the center is firm to the touch.
14. Cool on a wire rack for 30 minutes.
15. In a small saucepan, heat the raspberry jam over low heat until melted and smooth.
16. Spread the jam in an even layer on top of the cake.
17. Let stand until the cake is thoroughly cooled and the jam is set.
18. Loosen the sides of the cake with a small spatula.
19. Remove the sides of the pan and slide the cake onto a serving platter.
20. Decorate the top with a border of reserved chopped hazelnuts and evenly spaced raspberries.
21. Serve in thin wedges.
